item.title: [issue-open]: [Performance] Use official Docker image instead of installing on runner
item.author: lowlydba
item.content:encoded: ## Proposal: use the official Docker image instead of installing newsboat on the runnerIn my (short) experience, the Live Feed update workflow's `newsboat` install on GitHub-hosted runners is unreliable. The `snap` install frequently, and randomly, hangs for long periods (ex. this recent [14 minute run from this repo](https://github.com/exaroth/liveboat-github-runner/actions/runs/28669408354)), but completes in under a minute at other times.Since `ghcr.io/exaroth/liveboat` already bundles a compatible newsboat (Alpine apk, ~2.43) and the matching liveboat binary, running the whole build through that container avoids the install step entirely. I've had 5 consecutive test runs with this approach, each completed in under a minute, with zero flakiness. Even if this began to slow down, adding in caching for the docker image would likely help further - just doesn't seem worth it if this is stable already.I replaced the "Install newsboat" / "Fetch liveboat" / "Run newsboat" / "Run liveboat" steps with: - name: Run newsboat + liveboat docker run --rm \ -v "${{ github.workspace }}:/workspace" -w /workspace \ ghcr.io/exaroth/liveboat:latest \ sh -c "newsboat -u config/urls -C config/newsboat-config -c cache.db -x reload && liveboat --template-path templates/default --config-file config/liveboat-config.toml" sudo chown -R "$(id -u):$(id -g)" .> The container runs as root, so the `chown` after is needed to avoid git "dubious ownership" errors on the mounted workspace before the later commit/push steps.
